the former was treated with DES and the latter with BM stent

he denies any prior history of stroke, TIA, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding at the time of surgery, myalgias, joint pains, cough, hemoptysis, black stools or red stools

Gen: WDWN middle aged male in NAD, resp or otherwise. Oriented x3. Mood, affect appropriate. Pleasant.
HEENT: NCAT. Sclera anicteric. PERRL, EOMI. Conjunctiva were pink, no pallor or cyanosis of the oral mucosa.
Neck: Supple with JVP of cm.
CV: PMI located in 5th intercostal space, midclavicular line. RR, normal S1, S2. No S4, no S3.
Chest: No chest wall deformities, scoliosis or kyphosis. Resp were unlabored, no accessory muscle use. No crackles, wheeze, rhonchi.
Abd: soft, NTND, No HSM or tenderness. No abdominial bruits.
Ext: No c/c/e. No femoral bruits.
Skin: No stasis dermatitis, ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.
Pulses: Right: Carotid 2+ without bruit; Femoral 2+ without bruit; 2+ DP  Left: Carotid 2+ without bruit; Femoral 2+ without bruit; 2+ DP
Cardiac catherization
Coronary angiography in this right-dominant system revealed:  --the LMCA had a 20% ostial stenosis
--the LAD was totally occluded after D1.  D1 had a 60% ostial stenosis. There were right-to-left collaterals filling the LAD
the LCX had mild disease.
the RCA had a 90% proximal stenosis
Resting hemodynamics revealed elevated right- and left-sided filling pressures, with RVEDP 17 mmHg and mean PCWP 29 mmHg
There was moderate pulmonary arterial hypertension with PASP 52 mmHg
Systemic arterial systolic pressure was high-normal with SBP 138 mmHg
The cardiac output was preserved, with CI 2.3 L/min/m2.
Successful PTCA and stenting of the proximal right coronary artery with a Vision (4x12mm) bare metal stent postdilated with a 4mm balloon.
Final angiography demonstrated no angiographically apparent dissection, no residual stenosis and TIMI III flow
Successful PTCA and stenting of the mid left anterior descending artery with a Cypher (3x13mm) drug eluting stent postdilated with a 3mm balloon
Final angiography demonstrated no angiographically apparent dissection, no residual stenosis and TIMI III flow
Two-vessel CAD with ST elevation MI, likely proximal RCA culprit lesion with totally occluded LAD also noted (and filled by collaterals from RCA).
Elevated right- and left-sided filling pressures
Successful PTCA and stenting of the proximal right coronary artery with a bare metal stent
Successful PTCA and stenting of the mid LAD with a Cypher drug eluting stent
Chest X-ray
Supine positioning may be responsible in part for hilar and mediastinal vascular engorgement
Heart size is top normal
Lungs are clear and there is no appreciable pleural effusion
no appreciable pleural effusion
enlarged thyroid gland

Widening of the upper mediastinum indentation in the right side of the trachea at the thoracic inlet suggests an enlarged thyroid gland. Although a combination of mediastinal fat and tortuous vessels could give the same appearance.

multivessel CAD

The aortic valve leaflets (3) appear structurally normal with good leaflet excursion and no aortic regurgitation
no aortic regurgitation

trivial mitral regurgitation

His risk factors included hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and a family history
2 vessel disease on catherization,
and had a bare metal stent placed in his RCA and a DES to his LAD.

Based on history of his symptoms and findings on catherization and echo, it was suspected the patient suffered an acute event (occlusion of his RCA) in the setting of a prior event several weeks ago (occlusion of his LAD) when he had similar symptoms.
started on plavix, which he should continue for at least one year.

daily 325 mg aspirin
started on metoprolol and an ace-inhibitor as well, both of which were increased as tolerated by his heart rate and blood pressure

need to continue anticoagulation until a repeat echo in several months is completed to re-assess his LV function.
